data.title: India's NSE settles with regulator for $4.6 million over data sharing case
data.description:
India's largest bourse operator, National Stock Exchange of India, has settled a matter involving the sharing of confidential information of listed companies indirectly to a third-party vendor with the markets regulator for 403.5 million rupees ($4.6 million), the regulator said on Friday.

($1 = 87.4580 Indian rupees)

data.title: Ford Recalls 312,000 2025-Model Vehicles Over Power Brake Issues
data.description:
By Dean Seal

Ford Motor has recalled more than 312,000 of its recently manufactured vehicles over an issue that creates a loss of power-brake assist, according to the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration.

The recall affects certain 2025 Lincoln Navigator, F-150, Expedition, Bronco and Ranger vehicles, which have an electronic brake-booster module that may malfunction when driving or when the driver is using an assisted-driving system feature, said the NHTSA.

The loss of power-brake assist can extend stopping distance and increase the risk of a crash, and can result in a vehicle not braking as expected when the advanced driver assistance system is engaged, said the regulator.

The software will be updated either over-the-air or by a dealer for free, the NHTSA said. Notification letters will be mailed on Aug. 25.

Write to Dean Seal at dean.seal@wsj.com

data.title: PLEXUS CORP SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Plexus Corp, a leading provider of electronic manufacturing services, has released its latest Form 10-Q report, showcasing robust financial and operational performance for the quarter ended June 28, 2025. The company has demonstrated significant growth across various financial metrics and operational segments, driven by increased customer demand and successful production ramps of new products.

Financial Highlights

  • Net Sales: $1,018.3 million, increased by 6.0% compared to the same period last year, driven by increased customer demand and production ramps of new products.
  • Gross Profit: $103.3 million, representing a gross margin of 10.1%, up from 9.8% in the prior year, primarily due to operational efficiencies and prior restructuring activities.
  • Operating Income: $53.6 million, a 36.7% increase from the previous year, with an operating margin of 5.3%, reflecting a decrease in restructuring charges and improved gross profit.
  • Net Income: $45.1 million, up 79.7% from the previous year, attributed to higher operating income and reduced other expenses.
  • Diluted Earnings Per Share: $1.64, compared to $0.91 in the prior year, driven by increased net income.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: The company reported net sales across three primary market sectors: Aerospace/Defense, Healthcare/Life Sciences, and Industrial. The Healthcare/Life Sciences sector saw a notable increase of 10.6% in net sales for the three months ended June 28, 2025, driven by production ramps of new products and increased customer demand.
  • Geographical Performance: The APAC region experienced a significant increase in net sales by 13.9% for the three months ended June 28, 2025, attributed to increased customer demand and production ramps of new products. Conversely, the EMEA region saw a decrease in net sales by 14.3% due to decreased customer demand.
  • New Product Launches: The company focused on production ramps of new products for existing customers, particularly in the Aerospace/Defense and Healthcare/Life Sciences sectors, contributing to increased sales in these areas.
  • Future Outlook: Management anticipates continued growth in the APAC region due to ongoing production ramps and increased customer demand. The company is also focused on operational efficiency initiatives to further improve margins and profitability.

SEC Filing: PLEXUS CORP [ PLXS ] - 10-Q - Aug. 01, 2025

data.title: MERCANTILE BANK CORP SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
MERCANTILE BANK CORP, a prominent financial institution specializing in banking products and services within the United States, has released its Form 10-Q report for the second quarter of 2025. The report highlights significant financial and operational achievements, showcasing the company's robust performance and strategic initiatives.

Financial Highlights

Total Interest Income: $81.96 million for the three months ended June 30, 2025, reflecting an increase from $78.88 million in the same period in 2024, driven by higher interest income from loans and securities.

Net Interest Income: $49.48 million for the three months ended June 30, 2025, up from $47.07 million in the same period in 2024, indicating improved interest income after accounting for interest expenses.

Net Income: $22.62 million for the three months ended June 30, 2025, compared to $18.79 million in the same period in 2024, showcasing a significant increase in profitability.

Basic Earnings Per Share: $1.39 for the three months ended June 30, 2025, up from $1.17 in the same period in 2024, reflecting higher earnings distributed over a similar number of shares.

Diluted Earnings Per Share: $1.39 for the three months ended June 30, 2025, consistent with the basic earnings per share, indicating no significant dilution from potential shares.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: The company operates through a single business segment, deriving interest and noninterest income through banking products and services and investment securities. All income is related to operations in the United States.

Loan Portfolio Growth: Total loans increased by $97.2 million, or 2.1%, from December 31, 2024, to June 30, 2025. The commercial loan segment saw a 3.1% increase, while the retail loan segment experienced a 1.9% decrease.

Commercial Loan Performance: The commercial loan portfolio is divided into five segments based on collateral type and risk characteristics. The commercial and industrial loans increased by 6.8%, and real estate – multi-family and residential rental loans grew by 9.1%.

Retail Loan Performance: The 1-4 family mortgages segment decreased by 3.4%, while other consumer loans increased by 17.5%.

Nonaccrual Loans: Nonaccrual loans totaled $9.7 million as of June 30, 2025, with commercial loans accounting for $7.3 million and retail loans for $2.4 million.

Allowance for Credit Losses: The allowance for credit losses increased to $58.4 million as of June 30, 2025, reflecting a provision for credit losses of $3.7 million during the first six months of 2025.

Premises and Equipment: The company reported premises and equipment, net of $54.8 million as of June 30, 2025, with depreciation expense totaling $1.3 million during the second quarter of 2025.

Lease Agreements: The company is under lease contracts for eleven banking facilities, with a weighted average life of 9.3 years as of June 30, 2025. The right-of-use assets and lease liabilities totaled $4.2 million.

Deposits: Total deposits increased by $12.1 million, or 0.3%, from December 31, 2024, to June 30, 2025. Noninterest-bearing checking deposits decreased by 6.6%, while money market deposits increased by 5.0%.

Repurchase Agreements: The outstanding balance of securities sold under agreements to repurchase was $242.8 million as of June 30, 2025, with an average interest rate of 3.17%.

Federal Home Loan Bank Advances: FHLBI bullet advances totaled $330 million as of June 30, 2025, with maturities ranging from September 2025 through June 2030. The average interest rate was 3.25%.

Stock-Based Compensation: Stock-based compensation expense totaled $1.7 million during the six months ended June 30, 2025. The company granted 84,685 shares of restricted stock during this period.

Future Outlook: The company continues to focus on managing interest rate risk and maintaining a strong loan portfolio. The economic forecasts used for the allowance calculation reflected a $0.1 million allowance balance reduction as of June 30, 2025.

SEC Filing: MERCANTILE BANK CORP [ MBWM ] - 10-Q - Aug. 01, 2025

data.title: Liberty Global Ltd. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Liberty Global Ltd., a leading international telecommunications and television company, has released its Form 10-Q report for the second quarter of 2025. The report highlights significant financial and operational achievements, including robust revenue growth driven by strategic acquisitions and improved operational efficiency. However, the company also faces challenges such as inflationary pressures and competitive market conditions.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Revenue: $1,269.1 million, increased by 20.0% compared to the same period in 2024, driven by the impact of the Formula E Acquisition and Sunrise Services.
  • Operating Income: $29.6 million, compared to an operating loss of $33.0 million in the same period in 2024, reflecting improved operational efficiency.
  • Net Income: ($2,773.8 million), a significant decrease from $324.1 million in the same period in 2024, primarily due to non-operating expenses including foreign currency transaction losses.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: Telenet's revenue increased by 6.1% in the three months ended June 30, 2025, driven by a 0.6% organic increase, primarily due to higher residential fixed subscription revenue and other revenue streams.
  • Revenue Segments: VM Ireland experienced a 2.3% increase in revenue for the three months ended June 30, 2025, despite a 3.0% organic decrease, with B2B revenue showing growth.
  • Geographical Performance: The company operates in Belgium, Luxembourg, and Ireland, with significant noncontrolling interests in the U.K. and the Netherlands through joint ventures. The competitive environment and macroeconomic factors in these regions have impacted revenue and customer numbers.
  • Sales Units: As of June 30, 2025, the company served 11,412,000 fixed-line customers and 44,577,800 mobile subscribers across its networks.
  • New Product Launches: The company completed the acquisition of a controlling interest in Formula E on October 2, 2024, which has been consolidated into its operations, indicating a strategic expansion into new business areas.
  • Future Outlook: The company anticipates continued competition and regulatory challenges in its markets, which may impact revenue growth and customer retention. It is also facing inflationary pressures that could affect operating results and cash flows.
  • Operational Challenges: The company is experiencing inflationary pressures on labor, programming, and other costs, which could outpace revenue growth and negatively impact operating results.
  • Operational Strategy: The company is focused on managing rapid technological changes and maintaining competitive service offerings in broadband internet, video, fixed-line telephony, and mobile communications.

data.title: Cboe tops profit estimates as market volatility boosts hedging activity
data.description:
Derivatives exchange Cboe Global Markets beat Wall Street estimates for second-quarter profit on Friday, as looming economic uncertainties fueled market turmoil and boosted options trading.

Investors and portfolio managers rushed to hedge their positions during the period, responding to a spike in market volatility fueled by renewed geopolitical tensions in the Middle East and unpredictable tariff policies.

Cboe's options trading business revenue jumped 19% to $364.8 million, while Europe and Asia-Pacific revenue climbed 30% to $70 million.

The company has "achieved another quarter of record net revenue and strong adjusted earnings growth, highlighting the durability across our exchange ecosystem", said Chief Financial Officer Jill Griebenow.

Cboe wrapped up a strong quarter for U.S. exchanges such as CME Group , Nasdaq and Intercontinental Exchange . Heightened market volatility lifted trading volumes, marking a robust period of activity for the industry.

Average daily volume in index options hit 4.7 million contracts during the quarter ended June 30, compared with 4 million a year earlier.

Cboe's adjusted net income rose to $257.8 million, or $2.46 per share, from $226.2 million, or $2.15 per share, a year earlier. Analysts on average estimated $2.42 per share, according to data compiled by LSEG.

The company's net revenue rose 14% to a record high of $587.3 million, also beating the estimate of $576.2 million.

Last week, Cboe announced plans to wind down its Japanese equities business, citing challenges to financial sustainability.

Its shares have risen 23.4% this year, compared with a gain of 19.8% and 24%, for CME and NYSE-parent Intercontinental Exchange, respectively.

data.title: U.S. Dollar Surges as Scores of Countries Brace for Tariffs to Hit
data.description:
By Adrian Kerr

The dollar rose to a two-month high against a basket of major currencies after President Trump on Thursday said the U.S. would raise tariffs on multiple trading partners, starting on Aug. 7.

Expectations that tariffs will hurt the U.S. economy have dimmed in recent days even as jobs, inflation and growth data and the long-term rate path for the Federal Reserve remain open to interpretation.

A clear response to Trump's levies on Friday, though, was in foreign exchange markets, as major currencies plunged to multi-week lows against the U.S. dollar.

The planned tariffs include a 35% levy on Canadian goods that don't comply with the U.S.-Mexico-Canada trade agreement, and a 39% levy on Switzerland. The European Union, Japan and South Korea will get levies of 15% on their goods.

The U.S. currency hit a 10-week high of 1.3879 Canadian dollars and a 5-week peak of 0.8171 Swiss francs mid-way through the European trading day Friday.

"If this tariff [39% on Switzerland] were to remain in place it would knock around 0.6% off Swiss GDP and significantly more if the exemption on pharmaceutical goods is removed," said Adrian Prettejohn, Europe economist at Capital Economics in a note. The franc was "the weakest currency in the G10 FX space on Friday," said Kathleen Brooks, research director at XTB.

The euro slid to a seven-week low of $1.1390, LSEG data showed, amid concerns about the impact of tariffs on the eurozone economy. Sterling also fell to a 15-week low of $1.3138 with the Bank of England poised to cut its key rate again next week and with more cuts likely to come.

U.S. economic data have been strong of late, encouraging investors to move back into U.S. assets after the uncertainty provoked by Trump's announcement of plans for sweeping tariffs in April.

The DXY dollar index against a basket of major currencies rose to a two-month high, and could move higher still if non-farm payrolls figures for July come in strong. "Today's jobs data presents the best chance for the dollar to take an extra leap higher," ING currency analyst Francesco Pesole said.

Investment management company RBC BlueBay estimates that the U.S. has raised the average tariffs rate to approximately 18%, on a global basis. On this assumption, it projects annualized U.S. tariff revenues around $450 billion, versus $77 billion in 2024, an increase worth 1.25% of GDP. "This quantum of revenue should help lower the U.S. fiscal deficit slightly below 7% of GDP, in the year ahead," said BlueBay's Chief Investment Officer, Mark Dowding. "In the short term...this could suggest that a resurgent dollar has a bit further to run."

-Jessica Fleetham contributed to this article

Write to Adrian Kerr at adrian.kerr@dowjones.com

data.title: Carbon project developers call for complaints procedure overhaul
data.description:
Dozens of carbon project developers are urging the registries that set standards for the voluntary carbon market to overhaul their complaints procedures, following several high-profile cases that have dented trust in the sector.

The voluntary carbon market, which lets companies buy credits to offset their emissions, has been under growing scrutiny, with many environmental groups saying it generates "junk" credits that allow firms to "greenwash".

But industry advocates say complaints about projects, which have in some cases led registries such as U.S.-based Verra to stop them from generating credits, are causing unfair damage to the sector as a whole and holding up vital sources of funding for environmental and climate initiatives.

The Project Developers Forum, which represents more than 60 carbon projects from Kenya to Singapore, said current complaints procedures do not offer an effective way for developers to defend any alleged wrongdoing before it is made public.

"You see a lot of projects getting suspended and it becomes public very quickly," the forum's chair, Nick Marshall, told Reuters. "Integrity is non-negotiable, but how we surface and respond to allegations matters just as much."

Marshall also said a few bad actors were doing undue damage to the industry's reputation and that developers with knowledge of wrongdoing tended not to come forward because the whistle-blowing procedure was not sufficiently anonymised.

He said the group has asked leading registry Verra to develop a clear policy and secure submission form on its website which would allow information about possible integrity issues to be shared anonymously.

Verra told Reuters in a statement it has had a formal and robust process in place for submitting complaints since early 2024 and that it "already contains provisions that address the concerns that the PD Forum has raised".

Last year, Verra rejected as many as 37 low-emission rice cultivation projects located in China following a quality control review.

data.title: Lincoln National Corporation's Board of Directors Declares Series C and Series D Preferred Stock Dividends
data.description:
RADNOR, Pa.--(BUSINESS WIRE)--August 01, 2025--

Lincoln Financial announced today that the board of directors of Lincoln National Corporation has declared the following preferred stock dividends:

  • Semi-annual dividend of $1,156.25 per share on the corporation's 9.250% Fixed Rate Reset Non-Cumulative Preferred Stock, Series C, $25,000 liquidation preference per share, represented by depositary shares each representing a 1/25th interest in a share of the preferred stock, holders of which will receive $46.25 per share.
  • Quarterly dividend of $562.50 per share on the corporation's 9.000% Non-Cumulative Preferred Stock, Series D, $25,000 liquidation preference per share, represented by depositary shares each representing a 1/1,000th interest in a share of the preferred stock, holders of which will receive $0.5625 per depositary share (NYSE: LNC PRD).

The above dividends will be payable September 1, 2025 to holders of record on August 15, 2025.

About Lincoln Financial

Lincoln Financial helps people confidently plan for their vision of a successful financial future. As of December 31, 2024, approximately 17 million customers trust our guidance and solutions across four core businesses — annuities, life insurance, group protection, and retirement plan services. As of June 30, 2025, the company had $331 billion in end-of-period account balances, net of reinsurance. Headquartered in Radnor, PA., Lincoln Financial is the marketing name for Lincoln National Corporation and its affiliates. Learn more at LincolnFinancial.com.

data.title: CGX Energy Provides Notice of Second Quarter 2025 Financial Results and Update on Corentyne Block
data.description:
Toronto, Ontario--(Newsfile Corp. - August 1, 2025) - CGX Energy Inc. ("CGX" or the "Company") announced today that it will release its financial and operational results for the second quarter ended June 30, 2025, after markets close on Wednesday, August 13, 2025.

As previously disclosed on May 9, 2025, Frontera Energy Corporation and its subsidiaries, Frontera Petroleum International Holding B.V. and Frontera Energy Guyana Holding Ltd. (together, the "Investors"), submitted a notice of intent to the Government of Guyana on March 26, 2025. The notice alleges breaches of the United Kingdom - Guyana Bilateral Investment Treaty and the Guyana Investment Act by the Government of Guyana, and initiated a three-month consultation and negotiation period aimed at resolving the dispute amicably (the "Notice of Intent").

On July 23, 2025, the Government of Guyana, through its legal counsel, responded to the Investors, rejecting their claims regarding the Corentyne block license. The Government of Guyana reaffirmed its view that the Joint Venture's interest expired on June 28, 2024, but noted that it may consider a final meeting with the Investors, on a without prejudice basis, in October 2025, and the Joint Venture would be informed as to whether such a meeting will occur in September 2025.

Frontera Energy Guyana Corp. ("Frontera Guyana") and CGX Resources Inc. ("CGX Resources", and together, the "Joint Venture") remain firmly of the view that its interests in, and the license for, the Corentyne block remain in place and in good standing and that the Petroleum Agreement has not been terminated. Although the three-month consultation and negotiation period delivered from the Notice of Intent has now expired, the Joint Venture and its stakeholders continue to invite the Government of Guyana to amicably resolve the issues affecting the Joint Venture's investments in the Corentyne block. Should the parties not reach a mutually agreeable solution, the Joint Venture and its stakeholders are prepared to assert their legal rights.

The Joint venture holds a 100% working interest in the Corentyne block, located offshore Guyana. Frontera Guyana and CGX Resources have agreed that their respective participating interests are 72.52% and 27.48%, which includes a 4.52% interest which CGX Resources agreed to assign to Frontera Guyana in 2023. The assignment of this 4.52% participating interest remains subject to the approval of the Government of Guyana, but is believed to be enforceable between Frontera Guyana and CGX Resources.

About CGX

CGX is a Canadian-based oil and gas exploration company focused on the exploration of oil in the Guyana-Suriname Basin and the development of a deep-water port in Berbice, Guyana.

data.title: T. Rowe Price quarterly profit beats estimates on boost from market rally
data.description:
Asset manager T. Rowe Price beat second-quarter profit expectations on Friday as a rebound in equities boosted its assets under management and softened the hit from outflows.

WHY IT'S IMPORTANT

Stock markets bounced back sharply in the second half of the quarter after tariff-driven turmoil in April as progress on trade negotiations eased recession fears.

Assets under management mainly depend on two factors - the performance of investments and money flowing in and out of the funds.

KEY QUOTE

"We have developed a broad and ongoing plan to reduce our expense growth over time while continuing to invest in capabilities and client reach. We believe that our plan will drive efficiency to fund investment in the future of the business," CEO Rob Sharps said in a statement.

CONTEXT

Active asset managers like T. Rowe usually buy and sell investments more frequently than passive fund managers.

Such firms have ceded market share and grappled with consistent outflows in recent years due to the growing popularity of low-cost passive funds, which centers around investing in index-tracking funds.

BY THE NUMBERS

T. Rowe's assets under management jumped 6.9% to $1.68 trillion in the quarter despite net client outflows of $14.9 billion.

Investment advisory fees, typically a percentage of the AUM, came in flat at $1.57 billion.

On an adjusted basis, T. Rowe earned $2.24 per share in the quarter, beating Wall Street expectations of $2.13, according to estimates compiled by LSEG.

data.title: Imperial Oil Files 8K - Regulation FD >IMO
data.description:
Imperial Oil Ltd. (IMO) filed a Form 8K - Regulation FD Disclosure - with the U.S Securities and Exchange Commission on August 01, 2025.

On August 1, 2025, Imperial Oil Limited (the "company") by means of a press release announced that the board of directors declared a quarterly cash dividend of 72 cents per share on the outstanding common shares of the company for the third quarter of 2025. The dividend is payable on October 1, 2025, to shareholders of record at the close of business on September 4, 2025. A copy of the press release is attached as Exhibit 99.1 to this report.

The full text of this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/49938/000004993825000042/imo-20250801.htm

Any exhibits and associated documents for this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/49938/000004993825000042/0000049938-25-000042-index.htm

Public companies must file a Form 8-K, or current report, with the SEC generally within four days of any event that could materially affect a company's financial position or the value of its shares.

data.title: HR services provider Insperity reports Q2 net loss of $5 mln
data.description:
Overview

  • Insperity Q2 revenue rises 3% yr/yr but missed analyst expectations

  • Adjusted EPS for Q2 misses consensus, reflecting higher benefits costs

  • Co reports Q2 net loss of $5 mln, adjusted EBITDA misses estimates

Outlook

  • Insperity sees Q3 adjusted EPS between $0.06 and $0.49

  • Company expects full-year adjusted EPS of $1.81 to $2.51

  • Insperity forecasts Q3 adjusted EBITDA of $24 mln to $44 mln

  • Company projects full-year adjusted EBITDA of $170 mln to $205 mln

Result Drivers

  • HIGHER HEALTHCARE COSTS - Gross profit decline attributed to elevated healthcare costs, including pharmacy trends and large claim activity

  • REVENUE GROWTH - 3% revenue increase driven by higher pricing and slight increase in average paid WSEEs

  • BENEFIT PLAN CHANGES - Co implementing benefit plan design changes to address elevated benefits cost trend, per CFO James D. Allison
